spec_match.rationale: Touches core terms from the chromestatus summary but skips Map.prototype.getOrInsert, Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed, WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsert, WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, input); controls include "Run read-modify-write"; "Run upsert"; "Reset counter" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5201653661827072.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on concurrency-stress
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Upsert introduces Map.prototype.getOrInsert, Map.prototype.getOrInsertComputed, WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsert, WeakMap.prototype.getOrInsertComputed but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://github.com/tc39/proposal-upsert

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on concurrency-stress
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: concurrency-stress covers the happy path of Upsert and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
